Petition For Order Confirming Estate Assets
TENTATIVE RULING: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
EXAMINER: AERO FETH

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-06-2026 Continued To Jun-08-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Confirming Estate Assets
Off Calendar Apr-06-2026 Continued To Jun-08-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio for administrator Cynthia Broussard (via Zoom).
Matthew B. Talbot for petitioner and heir Benjamin Franklin (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Petition for Order Confirming Estate Assets; and 2) Status on Close of Estate.

The Court inquired regarding the status of a settlement. Mr. Talbot reported that a settlement had been reached and that funds were being held in his client trust account. Mr. Talbot stated that he had prepared a deed for the administrator to execute, but that it had not yet been signed and returned, and that the settlement did not require involvement of a title company. Mr. Talbot further stated that he intended to file a petition to remove the administrator if the deed was not executed.

Ms. Fisher-Kouadio reported that completion of the settlement required coordination with a title company, which had declined to proceed and required submission to another title company for review. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that additional information had been requested and that she was working to address those issues, and estimated that the matter could be completed within approximately 30 days.

After hearing from counsel, the Court continued the matter to June 8, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Continued To Apr-06-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio for administrator Cynthia Broussard (via Zoom).
Mark Miyasaki for petitioner Benjamin Franklin (via Zoom).
Rachel Dodson for Leslie Byrne, personal representative of the Estate of Rita (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Petition for Order Confirming Estate Assets; 2) Status on Close of Estate; and 3) Status of Mediation.

Counsel confirmed that the parties had settled and that an ex parte application to approve the settlement had been submitted. The Court advised that approval of the settlement should proceed by fully noticed petition absent a showing of good cause for ex parte relief. Mr. Miyasaki stated that a petition for approval of settlement could be filed within approximately 30 days. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that a petition for final distribution had been prepared but would be updated following court approval of the settlement.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to April 6, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 to allow time for a petition for approval of settlement to be filed. Status on mediation was taken off calendar.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order Confirming Estate Assets
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Continued To Apr-06-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-10-2025 Continued To Feb-23-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Confirming Estate Assets
Off Calendar Nov-10-2025 Continued To Feb-23-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio for administrator Cynthia Broussard (via Zoom).
Matthew B. Talbot for petitioner Benjamin Franklin (via Zoom).
Rachel Dodson for Leslie Byrne, personal representative of the Estate of Rita Franklin (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Petition for Order Confirming Estate Assets; and 2) Status on Close of Estate.

The Court inquired whether the parties had been able to reach an agreement through mediation. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio reported that the parties had previously participated in a pro bono mediation and executed a memorandum of understanding but were unable to finalize a settlement due to disagreement over administrative fees and coordination between this estate and related matters.

Counsel reported that a second round of mediation was scheduled for January 16, 2026 and requested that the matter be continued to a date following that session. The Court inquired whether a Breslin order should issue. Counsel agreed that issuing a Breslin order would be helpful.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to February 23, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. A hearing on Status of Mediation was set for the same date. The Court indicated that a Breslin order would be issued and sent to counsel.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order Confirming Estate Assets
Off Calendar Sep-22-2025 Continued To Nov-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Matthew Talbot for petitioner Benjamin Franklin, who was also present (via Zoom).
Rachel Dodson for Leslie Byrne, personal representative of the Estate of Rita Franklin, who was also present (via Zoom).
Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio for administrator Cynthia Broussard, who was also present.

The matter was called for hearing on the Petition for Order Confirming Trust Assets, and for status on close of estate.

The Court inquired into whether the parties participated in mediation. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio confirmed that mediation occurred, resulting in a memorandum of understanding, but final settlement documentation remained incomplete. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio discussed issues regarding dispute over administration fees in connection with property buyouts.

Mr. Talbot requested a continuance to allow time to resolve outstanding issues before the next hearing, and noted that most of the fee-related issues raised by Ms. Fisher-Kouadio were not relevant to his client's 850 petition and involved separate disputes.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to November 10, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Lydia Villarreal; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-22-2025 Continued To Nov-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-23-2025 Continued To Sep-22-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio, Esq., in court, for and with Cynthia Broussard, Petitioner.
Rachel Dodson, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for and with Leslie Byrne, Petitioner.
Mark Miyasaki, Esq. via Zoom videoconference, for Benjamin Franklin, Objector.
Chanee Franklin Minor, Esq. via Zoom videoconference, for Charles Franklin, in court, interested party.

Discussion was held about mediation, that parties could not all get together with the mediator due to scheduling issues, and that the 850 petition has been filed and to be heard on September 22, 2025.

All parties agree to extending the deadline for mediation.

The Court extends the date for mediation. Mediation must be completed by August 22, 2025. Counsels to file report of the status of mediation by August 29, 2025. The matter is continued to September 22, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Continued To Jun-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-24-2025 Continued To Apr-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio for Cynthia Broussard, Administrator (via Zoom).
Rachel Dodson for and with Leslie M. Byrne, Executor of the Estate of Rita Franklin (via Zoom).

Discussions were held regarding The Estate of Rita Franklin, which has been ready for closure since March 2024 but is pending the closure of the Estate of Pinkey Broussard. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that the primary issue delaying closure is the negotiation of buyouts between beneficiaries to retain property interests and avoid reassessment of property taxes. She explained the ongoing negotiations and difficulties in communicating with certain beneficiaries. Rachel Dalton maintained that she represents only the Executor of the Estate of Rita Franklin and not any of the individual beneficiaries.

Given the potential financial impact on the beneficiaries, the Court issued a Minute Order Referring Case to Pro Bono Mediation to facilitate discussions and potential agreements regarding buyout terms and property interests. Mediation must be completed no later than April 14, 2025. Counsel must notify the assigned Probate Examiner of the status of mediation by April 21, 2025. The status hearing on close of estate is continued to April 28, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-14-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio, Esq., in court, for and with Cynthia Broussard, Petitioner.
Rachel Dodson, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for and with Leslie Byrne, Petitioner.
Benjamin Franklin, in court, in propria persona, Objector.
Charles Franklin, in court, in propria persona, interested party.

Counsel, and Mr. Frankling presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the instant petitions for Letters of Administration, and objection to the same.

Upon conclusion of discussion, the court orders the following: The court granted the Corrected Petition for Letters of Administration. Cynthia Broussard is appointed Administrator of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $900,000. Isidoor Bornstein is appointed Probate Referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Feb-24-2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

The Petition for Letters of Administration filed by Leslie M. Byrne is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.
